senses.text: late 13c., "to go up, rise, mount (a horse)," from Old French amonter, from a mont "upward," literally "to the mountain," from Latin ad- "to" (see ad-) + montem (nominative mons) "mountain" (see mount (n.1)). Meaning "to rise in number or quality (so as to reach)" is from c. 1300. Related: Amounted; amounting.
